mappings.value: The integral from minus pi to pi of rho of theta omega t in theta equals one. The integration is over phase theta for each fixed natural frequency omega, so rho is normalized as a phase density.

Equation 8 is the normalization constraint that goes with the continuity equation. It says the oscillators of a given natural frequency are redistributed around the phase circle, but their total mass stays one.
This is why stationary solutions like Eq. 9 must be checked against normalization as well as the transport equation itself.
Use Eq. 8 whenever you introduce or manipulate a continuum density rho. It is the basic consistency condition that keeps rho interpretable as a probability density in phase.
